lines: Welcome. [long pause] Take a breath. [long pause] You've arrived at the end of this day, and you're here. [long pause] In the dark. [long pause] In the bed. [long pause] With whatever you brought with you.

Sometimes we can't sleep because something is sitting in our chest that we haven't looked at. [long pause] An emotion we pushed aside during the busy hours because there wasn't time... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] or because it felt too much... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] or because we just didn't want to. [long pause] It followed us anyway. [long pause] It followed us through the evening, through the brushing of teeth, through the turning off of lights. [long pause] It's here now, in the bed, under the covers. [long pause] You can feel it, even if you can't name it yet.

Tonight, we make room for it. [long pause] Not to fix it. [long pause] Not to analyze it until it makes sense. [long pause] Not to make it go away through force of will. [long pause] Just to be with it honestly. [long pause] And to discover that being with it is not as dangerous as it seems.
But first, we settle the body. [long pause] The body comes first.

Adjust your position. [long pause] Move your pillow, shift your hips, roll your shoulders until something says... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] okay. [long pause] I can be here for a while. [long pause] Not perfect. [long pause] Just okay.

Notice where your body touches the bed. [long pause] The back of your head, cupped by the pillow. [long pause] Your shoulder blades, pressing into the mattress. [long pause] The curve of your lower back. [long pause] The weight of your hips. [long pause] The length of your legs, heavy against the sheets. [long pause] Your heels at the very end, resting.

These are the places where gravity holds you. [long pause] You don't have to hold yourself up. [long pause] The bed does that. [long pause] Gravity does that. [long pause] Physics, tonight, is on your side. [long pause] Let it work. [long pause] Surrender your weight to the surface beneath you.

Soften your jaw. [long pause] Let your teeth part so there's a thin gap between them. [long pause] Drop your tongue from the roof of your mouth... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] let it rest, thick and warm, at the bottom. [long pause] Smooth your forehead. [long pause] Feel the skin of your brow relax and widen. [long pause] Let the tiny muscles around your eyes go slack... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the ones that squint and furrow and express all day long. [long pause] They can stop now. [long pause] The day is not watching.

Let your hands open. [long pause] If they're clenched or curled, let the fingers loosen. [long pause] Palms up or palms down, it doesn't matter. [long pause] Just open. [long pause] Unclenched. [long pause] Receiving instead of gripping.

Now, breathe. [long pause] In through your nose for four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four. [long pause] And out through your mouth for eight... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] seven... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] eight. [long pause] Feel how the long exhale changes something in your chest. [long pause] A loosening. [long pause] A small surrender.

Again. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four. [long pause] And out... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] slow, like steam rising from warm skin in cold air... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] seven... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] eight.

One more. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and out, all the way to empty. [long pause] Good. [long pause] Let your breath find its own rhythm. [long pause] You've pointed it in the right direction. [long pause] It knows the way from here.

Tonight we practice something called RAIN. [long pause] It's a framework from the contemplative psychology tradition, adapted beautifully for moments like this one — moments when something is present in you that won't quiet down on its own. [long pause] Four steps. [long pause] Recognize. [long pause] Allow. [long pause] Investigate. [long pause] Nurture. [long pause] We'll take each one slowly, with more time than you might expect, because these things can't be rushed. [long pause] The feeling took all day to arrive. [long pause] We can give it more than thirty seconds.

The first step is simply noticing what's here. [long pause] Not what you think should be here. [long pause] Not what would be reasonable to feel. [long pause] Not what you wish you felt. [long pause] What is actually present, right now, in this body, in this bed, at this hour.

Scan your body gently. [long pause] Start at the top of your head and move downward... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] slowly... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] like lowering a lantern into a well. [long pause] Notice wherever something catches the light. [long pause] Is there tightness in your forehead — a band of pressure across the brow? [long pause] [long pause] A clenching in your jaw that came back even after you released it? [long pause] [long pause] A knot between your shoulder blades, as though the muscles there are still bracing for something?

Move lower. [long pause] Is there heaviness in your chest... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a weight that doesn't correspond to anything physical? [long pause] [long pause] A tightness in the solar plexus, that soft spot below the ribs? [long pause] [long pause] A churning in your stomach, or a hollowness? [long pause] [long pause] A restlessness in your legs that makes you want to move, to get up, to pace?

Whatever you find... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] wherever the lantern catches something... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] pause there. [long pause] That's information. [long pause] The body stores what the mind doesn't process. [long pause] You're reading the residue of the day, the week, maybe longer. [long pause] The body has been holding this for you. [long pause] It's been waiting for you to notice.

Now, try to name the feeling underneath the sensation. [long pause] Not the story — not the reason or the cause or whose fault it is. [long pause] Just the feeling itself. [long pause] One or two words.

Anxious. [long pause] Sad. [long pause] Frustrated. [long pause] Ashamed. [long pause] Lonely. [long pause] Overwhelmed. [long pause] Angry. [long pause] Numb. [long pause] Disappointed. [long pause] Afraid. [long pause] Hurt. [long pause] Tired in a way that has nothing to do with sleep.

Whatever word arrived, even if it surprised you, even if it doesn't fully capture the thing... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] that's close enough. [long pause] You've recognized it. [long pause] You've turned toward it instead of away. [long pause] That is the first step, and it takes more courage than most people realize. [long pause] Most people fall asleep every night without ever asking themselves what they're feeling. [long pause] You asked.

This is the step where most of us get stuck, because everything in us wants to fix, change, or escape difficult feelings. [long pause] The habit is deep. [long pause] When something hurts, we push it away. [long pause] When something is uncomfortable, we distract ourselves. [long pause] When something feels wrong, we try to make it right. [long pause] But tonight... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] just for this practice... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] we're going to try something different.

Instead of pushing this feeling away, or analyzing it, or bargaining with it, or shaming yourself for having it... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] can you simply allow it to be here? [long pause] [long pause] Not forever. [long pause] Not as a permanent resident. [long pause] Just for now. [long pause] Just for these few minutes.
Say silently to yourself: This is here. [long pause] I don't have to fight it.
This is here. [long pause] I don't have to fight it.

Allowing doesn't mean approving. [long pause] This is important. [long pause] Allowing doesn't mean you want this feeling, or that it's right, or that it deserves to stay, or that it gets the final word about who you are. [long pause] It means you stop fighting what is already here. [long pause] You stop spending energy pushing against something that is already inside you. [long pause] That fight is exhausting. [long pause] It burns energy you need for sleep, for healing, for tomorrow. [long pause] When you stop the fight... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] something in the chest loosens. [long pause] Something in the shoulders drops.

Notice what happens in your body when you stop resisting. [long pause] Does your breathing deepen slightly? [long pause] [long pause] Does your jaw unclench again? [long pause] [long pause] Does the feeling itself shift, even a little... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] becoming less sharp, less urgent, now that it's not being attacked?
Say it once more, and mean it as much as you can: This belongs to this moment. [long pause] I can let it be here without letting it be everything.

Now, gently, with the curiosity of someone who genuinely cares, move toward this feeling. [long pause] Not to interrogate it. [long pause] Not to make it explain itself. [long pause] Not to fix it. [long pause] Just to know it a little better. [long pause] The way you'd approach a friend who is hurting... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] sitting down beside them, not demanding they talk, just being close enough that they could.

If at any point this feels too intense, return your attention to the sensation of breathing. [long pause] The weight of your body on the bed. [long pause] The feeling of your head on the pillow. [long pause] You can always come back to this later. [long pause] That's not failure — that's wisdom.

Where does this feeling live in your body? [long pause] [long pause] Can you locate its center? [long pause] [long pause] Is it in your chest... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a pressure behind the sternum? [long pause] [long pause] In your throat... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a tightness, like words that were never said? [long pause] [long pause] In your stomach... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a churning, a hollowness, a heat? [long pause] [long pause] Behind your eyes... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a sting that might be tears if you let it?

Stay with the location. [long pause] Don't move away. [long pause] Breathe into that spot. [long pause] Imagine your breath flowing directly to wherever the feeling lives, surrounding it with warmth and air.

What shape does this feeling have? [long pause] [long pause] If you could see it, what would it look like? [long pause] [long pause] A tight knot... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a dark stone... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a heavy fog... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a burning coal... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] something jagged... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] something round and dense... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] something that shifts and changes when you look at it directly?

Does it have a temperature? [long pause] [long pause] Is it hot... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] restless... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] burning? [long pause] [long pause] Or cold... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] heavy... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] leaden? [long pause] [long pause] Or something in between... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a dull warmth, like a bruise?
Does it have a color? [long pause] [long pause] Don't think about it... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] just let a color come. [long pause] Whatever appears.

Now... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] ask this feeling a question. [long pause] Not out loud. [long pause] Silently. [long pause] Gently. [long pause] As though you're speaking to the most vulnerable part of yourself.
What would you say if you could speak?

Listen. [long pause] Don't rush to answer. [long pause] Don't rush to fix. [long pause] Just listen to whatever comes. [long pause] It might be a word... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] help. [long pause] It might be a memory... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] something unfinished, someone you miss. [long pause] It might be an image, or a sensation, or nothing clear at all. [long pause] All of it is valid. [long pause] The listening is what matters, not the answer.

Sometimes the feeling doesn't need a solution. [long pause] It doesn't need to be figured out. [long pause] It just needs to be heard. [long pause] Like a child who stops crying not because the problem was solved... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] but because someone knelt down, looked them in the eye, and listened. [long pause] That's what you've just done. [long pause] You knelt down inside yourself and listened.

This is the last step, and for many people, the hardest one. [long pause] Not because it's complicated... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] but because it asks you to be kind to yourself. [long pause] Genuinely kind. [long pause] Not in a saccharine way. [long pause] Not "you're amazing and everything is fine." Kind in the way that actually helps... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] which is honest, and tender, and close.

If it feels natural, place a hand on your heart. [long pause] Or on your stomach. [long pause] Or on your chest. [long pause] Wherever the feeling seems to live. [long pause] Feel the warmth of your own hand against your body. [long pause] The weight of it. [long pause] The realness of one part of you comforting another.

Now speak to yourself the way you would speak to someone you love who was hurting. [long pause] Not advice. [long pause] Not solutions. [long pause] Not analysis. [long pause] Just presence. [long pause] Just tenderness.
I see you. [long pause] This is hard. [long pause] You're not alone in it.
I see you. [long pause] You've been carrying this all day. [long pause] Maybe longer. [long pause] You can set it down now.
This pain is real. [long pause] And it doesn't define you. [long pause] It's something you're feeling. [long pause] It's not something you are.

"For I am convinced that neither death nor life, neither angels nor demons, neither the present nor the future, nor any powers, neither height nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God that is in Christ Jesus our Lord." Romans eight, verses thirty-eight and thirty-nine.

Listen to what Paul does here. [long pause] He makes a list. [long pause] He tries to think of everything... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] death, life, angels, demons, the present, the future, every power in existence, the highest height, the lowest depth... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and then, just in case he missed something, he adds "nor anything else in all creation." He is saying: I have searched the entire universe for something that could cut you off from God's love, and I cannot find it. [long pause] It does not exist.

Nothing you feel tonight separates you from that love. [long pause] Not the anxiety. [long pause] Not the sadness. [long pause] Not the shame. [long pause] Not the anger. [long pause] Not the numbness. [long pause] Not the feeling you can't name. [long pause] Nothing. [long pause] His grace is sufficient... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] not because your feeling is small, but because His love is larger than anything you carry.

Let the feeling rest now. [long pause] It has been recognized... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] named, located, seen. [long pause] It has been allowed... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] given space instead of war. [long pause] It has been investigated... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] approached with curiosity and care. [long pause] It has been nurtured... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] met with tenderness and with the promise that nothing can separate you from love.
The feeling can rest now. [long pause] And so can you.
We move from the heart to the body now. [long pause] Gently. [long pause] Slowly. [long pause] Letting the body follow the heart into quiet.
Bring your awareness to the top of your head. [long pause] Feel the scalp soften. [long pause] The forehead smooth. [long pause] The space between the eyebrows widen.
Your eyes resting behind closed lids. [long pause] Your cheeks. [long pause] Your jaw, loose and heavy. [long pause] Your lips slightly parted.

Your neck, releasing its last tensions. [long pause] Your throat, open and unguarded. [long pause] Your shoulders, dropping away from your ears one more time. [long pause] Farther. [long pause] Farther. [long pause] There.
Your arms, from shoulders to elbows to wrists to fingertips. [long pause] Warm and dense. [long pause] Heavy against the mattress. [long pause] Your hands open, receiving nothing, needing nothing.

Your chest, rising and falling with the quiet tide of breath. [long pause] Your heart beating behind the ribs, steady and faithful. [long pause] It doesn't need your attention. [long pause] It has been doing this since before you were born.
Your stomach, soft. [long pause] Your lower back, wide against the bed. [long pause] Your hips, settled into the mattress with their full weight.

Your thighs, heavy and warm. [long pause] Your knees. [long pause] Your shins and calves. [long pause] Your ankles. [long pause] The tops of your feet. [long pause] The soles of your feet, warm and still. [long pause] Your toes.

The whole body. [long pause] Present. [long pause] Heavy. [long pause] Warm. [long pause] Complete. [long pause] You have visited every part and every part has been given permission to stop. [long pause] The body is at rest.

Lord... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] thank you for meeting us in the difficult feelings, not just the easy ones. [long pause] Thank you that you don't ask us to clean up before we come to you. [long pause] We bring you what we found tonight... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the unnamed thing, the heavy thing, the thing we carried all day without knowing it. [long pause] You knew about it before we did. [long pause] Hold it while we sleep. [long pause] We trust you with the parts of ourselves we don't fully understand. [long pause] And we trust that nothing... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] nothing in all creation... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] separates us from your love. [long pause] Amen.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

The feeling has been seen. [long pause] The body is at rest. [long pause] Love surrounds you... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] not because you earned it, not because you performed well enough, but because nothing in all creation can stop it from reaching you.
Heavy and warm. [long pause] Breathing easy. [long pause] The night is deep and quiet around you.
Recognized. [long pause] Allowed. [long pause] Investigated. [long pause] Nurtured. [long pause] You did the honest work tonight. [long pause] Now let it go. [long pause] Let all of it go.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
Loved. [long pause] Completely. [long pause] As you are. [long pause] Right now. [long pause] In this bed. [long pause] With everything you carry.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
